title: Arsenal FC vs. AFC Bournemouth
description: This event is for the upcoming Premier League game, scheduled for Saturday, April 11, 2026 between Arsenal FC and AFC Bournemouth.

markets.question: Will Arsenal FC win on 2026-04-11?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 11, 2026
If Arsenal FC wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bournemouth's 2-1 upset victory over Arsenal at the Emirates Stadium in Premier League Matchweek 32 on April 11 drives trader consensus to 100% implied probability on the Cherries' win, reflecting the confirmed result amid Arsenal's faltering title push. Junior Kroupi's early strike and Bournemouth's clinical finishing capitalized on Arsenal's injury absences—including Saka, Ødegaard, and Calafiori—fatigue, and rust from their first league game since mid-March, disrupting rhythm despite a response goal. Mikel Arteta labeled it a "big punch on the face," dropping points against a mid-table 13th-placed side historically outmatched by Arsenal. Official score ratification seals the outcome, with negligible risk of reversal barring unprecedented VAR overrule or abandonment.

title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Spain at a 15.4% implied probability to claim the 2026 FIFA World Cup title, with France (14.6%) and England (11.3%) nipping closely at their heels amid a bunched top tier that underscores the tournament's parity. The full 48-team field locked in after European playoffs concluded March 31—sending Bosnia and Herzegovina, Sweden, Turkiye, and Czechia through while Italy shockingly missed out—confirmed all elite contenders' presence, including Argentina and Brazil, whose qualifier table dominance and five-time champion pedigree keep them competitive. Spain's edge stems from Euro 2024 triumph, youthful stars like Lamine Yamal, and a favorable Group H draw against Cabo Verde and others, but the expanded format's group stage volatility, delayed top clashes until semifinals, and no major injury disruptions foster a tight race among UEFA and CONMEBOL heavyweights.

title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket places California Governor Gavin Newsom as the frontrunner at 27.5% implied probability for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination, driven by his executive experience, national media presence as a Trump critic, and vast fundraising network from tech and Hollywood donors, despite California's governance challenges. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez trails at 8.6% on her progressive appeal and social media savvy, while Jon Ossoff's 6.7% reflects swing-state Senate success and moderate positioning. Over the past week, figures like Kamala Harris signaled 2028 interest at the National Action Network convention in New York, courting Black voters amid concerns over electability. With 2026 midterms looming, strong gubernatorial or Senate results, early polls, and party endorsements could consolidate support behind a leader.

title: The Masters 2026: Winner
description:
This polymarket will resolve according to the player who wins the 2026 Masters Tournament.

If this player is eliminated from contention for The Masters based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by The Masters official tournament rules.

If no winner is announced by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution sources will be the official results published by the PGA TOUR website and The Masters website. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

If this player is eliminated from contention for The Masters based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by The Masters official tournament rules.

If no winner is announced by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution sources will be the official results published by the PGA TOUR website and The Masters website. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rory McIlroy's commanding six-shot lead at 12-under-par after a historic second-round 65—featuring birdies on six of his final seven holes—has solidified trader consensus at 69.5% implied probability for him to claim back-to-back green jackets as defending champion, the first since Tiger Woods in 2001-02. This marks the largest 36-hole advantage in Masters history at Augusta National amid fast, firm conditions. Patrick Reed (5.0%) and Sam Burns (3.6%), both at six under, trail as top challengers with Reed's 2018 victory and strong short game providing upset potential, while Tommy Fleetwood (4.6%) and Justin Rose (3.8%) lurk further back on solid ball-striking. Round 3 tee times and any weather shifts could test the field, but McIlroy's form and course history dominate sentiment.

title: Military action against Iran ends on...?
description:
This market will resolve according to the next date Iran Standard Time (GMT+3:30), on which neither the US nor Israel initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Iranian soil or any official Iranian embassy or consulate. 

If the date/time of a qualifying strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar day after the listed date, the respective market will resolve to "Yes" regardless of whether a strike is later confirmed to have occurred. If strikes could be verified under the prior rule for each listed day, this market will resolve to "Military action through April 30."

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by US or Israeli military forces that impact Iranian ground territory or any official Iranian embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Iranian soil is hit by an US/Israeli missile, this will qualify).

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be considered, regardless of whether they land on Iranian territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US/Israeli ground operatives will not be considered.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by US or Israeli military forces that impact Iranian ground territory or any official Iranian embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Iranian soil is hit by an US/Israeli missile, this will qualify).

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be considered, regardless of whether they land on Iranian territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US/Israeli ground operatives will not be considered.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

series.title: Military action against Iran ends on

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices April 9 at 98.5% implied probability for the end of military action against Iran, driven by the US-Iran ceasefire agreement announced April 7-8 and taking effect that week, halting US and Israeli airstrikes following six weeks of intense exchanges since late February. No verified strikes on Iranian soil have occurred since, with CENTCOM now clearing Iranian sea mines from the Strait of Hormuz as post-conflict stabilization. The two-week pause holds amid Pakistan-mediated talks, despite strains from Israeli strikes on Iranian proxies in Lebanon, which fall outside the deal. Resuming hostilities—via Iranian retaliation, Hormuz disruptions, or proxy escalations—could challenge this, though trader sentiment reflects de-escalation momentum and Iran's degraded military capabilities.

title: Counter-Strike: Astralis vs FUT Esports (BO5) - PGL Bucharest Playoffs
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Grand final match between Astralis and FUT Esports in the PGL Bucharest Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 11 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Astralis" if Astralis win the match against FUT Esports.

This market will resolve to "FUT Esports" if FUT Esports win the match against Astralis.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Counter-Strike: Astralis vs FUT Esports - Map 4 Winner
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Grand final match between Astralis and FUT Esports in the PGL Bucharest Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 11 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Astralis" if Astralis win Map 4 against FUT Esports.

This market will resolve to "FUT Esports" if FUT Esports win Map 4 against Astralis.

If the match begins but is not completed, and Map 4 is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on the completed Map 4. 

If Map 4 is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Games Total: O/U 3.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Grand final match between Astralis and FUT Esports in the PGL Bucharest Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 11 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if Astralis and FUT Esports play 4 or more maps in this series.

If fewer than 4 maps are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

markets.question: Map 2: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If Map 3 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 3 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 3 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Map 4: Odd/Even Total Rounds?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined rounds played by both teams in Map 4 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined rounds played by both teams in Map 4 is an even number.

"Total combined rounds" is the sum of rounds won by both teams during the match, including any overtime rounds if applicable. For example, a match ending 13–11 would have a total of 24 rounds (even), while a match ending 13–10 would have a total of 23 rounds (odd).

If Map 4 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 4 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 4 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 4 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 4 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Map 5: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 5 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 5 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 5, or if Map 5 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 5 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 5 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 5 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 5 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Map Handicap: AST (-2.5) vs FUT Esports (+2.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Grand final match between Astralis and FUT Esports in the PGL Bucharest Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 11 at 10:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Astralis" if Astralis wins 3 or more maps than FUT Esports in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "FUT Esports". 

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Astralis clashes with surging FUT Esports in the PGL Bucharest 2026 CS2 playoffs grand final BO5, both teams undefeated through the Swiss group stage and dominant semifinals—Astralis edging 3DMAX 2-0 on tense Dust2 (13-11) and Overpass (13-10) maps via phzy's MVP clutches (1.47 rating), FUT dispatching The MongolZ 2-0 convincingly. Astralis (#10 HLTV, #3 Valve) boasts a lopsided 6-1 head-to-head record over recent BO3s, including EPL triumphs, leveraging veteran roster stability against FUT's youthful firepower (average age 19.3, #13 HLTV/#6 Valve) on a five-match win streak with balanced 1.10 ratings. LAN play at PGL Studios emphasizes map vetoes, utility execution, and economy rounds in this $1.25M showdown.

eventMetadata.context_description: Liverpool's 94.5% implied probability as heavy favorite stems from their dominant home record at Anfield—winning 12 of the last 19 Premier League meetings against Fulham—coupled with superior squad depth and recent returns of Mohamed Salah to the starting XI and Alexander Isak for limited minutes off the bench following long-term absences. Arne Slot's latest injury update confirms Alisson Becker, Conor Bradley, Wataru Endo, and others sidelined, but Giorgi Mamardashvili provides reliable cover in goal, while Fulham miss key defender Kenny Tete (ankle), midfielder Harrison Reed (knee), and forward Kevin (foot), hampering their mid-table challenge amid Rodrigo Muniz's recent scoring form. Trader sentiment reflects Liverpool's top-four push and quality edge, though defensive lapses, further injuries like Jeremie Frimpong's concern, or Fulham's counter-attacks could spark an upset or draw.

title: Next Prime Minister of Hungary
description:
Parliamentary elections are expected to be held in Hungary on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve to the individual who is next officially appointed and confirmed as Prime Minister of Hungary following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally elected and appointed to the role of Prime Minister. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is confirmed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Hungary; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: With Hungary's parliamentary election on April 12 determining the next prime minister, trader consensus on Polymarket prices Péter Magyar of the Tisza party at 70.5% implied probability, reflecting polling averages like Politico's 50% Tisza versus 39% Fidesz and seat projections showing Tisza poised for a slim 104-seat majority in the 199-seat National Assembly. Recent developments include massive opposition rallies, such as tens of thousands at Budapest's Heroes' Square on April 10, signaling high anti-incumbent turnout against Viktor Orbán after 16 years in power. Mixed late polls—Fidesz leading 50-52% to Tisza's 39-41% in independents like Minerva, but Tisza ahead in others—highlight uncertainty, with Orbán's 29.5% odds sustained by incumbency advantages, rural strength, and the mixed-member electoral system favoring larger parties. Minor candidates trail far behind.

title: US x Iran diplomatic meeting by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify. 

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran, and a consensus of credible reporting.

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran, and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Direct US-Iran negotiations began April 11, 2026, in Islamabad, Pakistan, with delegations discussing an end to recent hostilities under a fragile ceasefire agreed April 7 following weeks of military escalation, including US threats to strike Iranian infrastructure and Israeli actions in Lebanon. This marks a shift from prior indirect contacts via Oman and mediators, amid Trump's extended deadlines on Strait of Hormuz access and Iran's demands for asset releases and nuclear enrichment rights. Sticking points include ceasefire compliance allegations and regional proxies, with talks hosted by Pakistan potentially yielding de-escalation or breakdown; trader focus remains on near-term breakthroughs before April 14 resolution windows.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder lead trader consensus at 40.5% implied probability for the 2026 NBA championship, bolstered by their league-best 64-17 record, recent clinching of the No. 1 overall playoff seed, and status as defending champions after winning in 2025, with Shai Gilgeous-Alexander anchoring MVP-caliber play alongside Chet Holmgren's rim protection. San Antonio Spurs sit second at 16.1% following a 62-19 mark and a perfect February unbeaten streak driven by Victor Wembanyama's dominance, positioning them for a favorable Western Conference path. Boston Celtics (11.9%) hold steady as East No. 2 seeds at 55-26 amid consistent form, while Denver Nuggets (10.5%) surge on an 11-game win streak to 53-28 despite a recent Thunder rout. Detroit Pistons (5.8%) top the East at 59-22, fueling upset potential as playoffs loom April 18.

title: Bitcoin above ___ on April 11?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

title: FC Barcelona vs. RCD Espanyol de Barcelona
description: This event is for the upcoming La Liga game, scheduled for Saturday, April 11, 2026 between FC Barcelona and RCD Espanyol de Barcelona.

markets.question: Will FC Barcelona win on 2026-04-11?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 11, 2026
If FC Barcelona wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

eventMetadata.context_description: Barcelona's commanding 97.3% implied probability stems from their atop-La-Liga position with 76 points from 30 matches, a seven-point lead over Real Madrid, contrasted against mid-table Espanyol's 38 points and poor recent form, including no wins in their last five La Liga outings. Traders price in Barcelona's dominance in the Catalan derby, with four straight victories including a 2-0 win in January's reverse fixture, bolstered by home advantage at Camp Nou and head-to-head superiority (33 wins in 52 meetings). Hansi Flick's squad features Gavi's return and Frenkie de Jong's recovery from hamstring absence, though rotation looms for fatigued stars like Pedri and Lamine Yamal amid a congested schedule. Upsets remain possible via Espanyol counterattacks, red cards, or unexpected injuries to key Blaugrana like Lewandowski, but historical patterns and current momentum heavily favor the hosts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance leads Polymarket's 2028 presidential winner odds at 18.8% implied probability, buoyed by his March 28 CPAC straw poll triumph with 53% GOP support and a recent UMass Lowell poll showing him ahead of Gavin Newsom 33%-30% in a hypothetical matchup. California Governor Newsom trails closely at 16.2%, bolstered by his tied second-place finish in a March New Hampshire Democratic primary poll and national visibility as a post-Harris contender. Secretary of State Marco Rubio's 9.8% reflects rising GOP appeal amid Trump administration roles. The tight race persists due to the long timeline before 2028 primaries, fragmented fields, and uncertainty from upcoming 2026 midterms in swing states, where gubernatorial or Senate gains could elevate challengers or solidify incumbency advantages.

title: LoL: G2 Esports vs Team Vitality (BO3) - LEC Regular Season
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between G2 Esports and Team Vitality in the LEC Regular Season, initially scheduled for April 11 at 10:15AM ET.

This market will resolve to "G2 Esports" if G2 Esports win the match against Team Vitality.

This market will resolve to "Team Vitality" if Team Vitality win the match against G2 Esports.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Games Total: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between G2 Esports and Team Vitality in the LEC Regular Season, initially scheduled for April 11 at 10:15AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if G2 Esports and Team Vitality play 3 or more games in this series.

If fewer than 3 games are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both G2 Esports and Team Vitality each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both G2 Esports and Team Vitality each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both G2 Esports and Team Vitality each destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) do not destroy any enemy inhibitors.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.question: Game 1: Any Player Quadra Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. In League of Legends, a Quadra Kill occurs when a single player kills 4 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Quadra Kill. A Penta Kill (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as a Quadra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Quadra Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Quadra Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 1 is an even number.

Executions (deaths to non-champion sources such as turrets, minions, or neutral monsters where no enemy champion receives kill credit) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

markets.question: Game 2: Any Player Penta Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Penta Kill during Game 2. In League of Legends, a Penta Kill occurs when a single player kills all 5 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Penta Kill during Game 2. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Penta Kill.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Penta Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Penta Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 2: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 2 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 2 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all champion kills recorded during the match by both G2 Esports and Team Vitality. Executions (deaths to non-champion sources such as turrets, minions, or neutral monsters where no enemy champion receives kill credit) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Game 2, or if Game 2 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.question: Game 3: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 3. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both G2 Esports and Team Vitality each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 3. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 3.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.question: Game 3: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 3. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both G2 Esports and Team Vitality each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 3. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 3. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

markets.question: Game Handicap: VIT (-1.5) vs G2 Esports (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between G2 Esports and Team Vitality in the LEC Regular Season, initially scheduled for April 11 at 9:55AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Team Vitality" if Team Vitality wins 2 or more games than G2 Esports in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "G2 Esports". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Team Vitality enters as the LEC Spring Split regular season leaders with a 4-1 series record (9-4 games) and a four-win streak, fueled by recent BO3 victories over Movistar KOI (2-1), Natus Vincere (2-0), and Team Heretics (2-0), showcasing strong macro from jungler Lyncas and ADC Carzzy. G2 Esports languishes mid-table at 1-2 (4-4 games) following a 1-2 loss to Fnatic last weekend, despite a prior 2-0 win over Heretics, with recent inconsistencies testing new jungler SkewMond alongside midlane veteran Caps. G2 holds a recent head-to-head edge (3-2 over five meetings) and 81% scrim winrate versus Vitality, heightening upset potential in this pivotal BO3 for playoff positioning amid roster-locked lineups.

title: Fed decision in April?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders overwhelmingly price a 98.3% implied probability of no change in the Fed funds rate at the April 28-29 FOMC meeting, driven by hotter-than-expected March 2026 CPI rising 3.3% year-over-year—up from February's 2.4%—and resilient labor data showing unemployment dipping to 4.3% with 178,000 nonfarm payroll gains, exceeding forecasts. This reinforces the Federal Reserve's March pause amid firm economic growth and sticky inflation, aligning with CME FedWatch Tool consensus above 97%. Hawkish momentum has pushed year-ahead rate cut odds below 20%, though a surprise downturn in upcoming April CPI (due mid-May) or softening jobless claims could marginally revive easing expectations before resolution.

title: Republican Presidential Nominee 2028
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Republican Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Republican Party sources.

Any replacement of the Republican nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump win the 2028 Republican presidential nomination?

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket favors Robert F. Kennedy Jr. at 49% implied probability to secure the 2028 Republican presidential nomination, edging Vice President J.D. Vance at 40.1% and Secretary of State Marco Rubio at 21.7%, in a wide-open field barred to President Trump by two-term limits under the 22nd Amendment. Recent momentum stems from Rubio's betting odds surge last week amid praised diplomatic efforts, while Vance confronts tensions between his past anti-forever war rhetoric and the administration's ongoing Iran operations, as noted in late March reporting; a March 28 CPAC straw poll showed Vance leading with Rubio advancing, underscoring a polling-market divergence. With no formal declarations, 2026 midterms loom as a key test of factional strength in swing states and primaries.

title: Brentford FC vs. Everton FC
description: This event is for the upcoming Premier League game, scheduled for Saturday, April 11, 2026 between Brentford FC and Everton FC.

markets.question: Will Brentford FC win on 2026-04-11?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 11, 2026
If Brentford FC wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. eventMetadata.context_description: The Polymarket market for Brentford FC vs. Everton FC has resolved to Draw at 100% after the Premier League clash at Gtech Community Stadium ended 2-2 on April 11. Igor Thiago's brace for Brentford—including an early penalty—put the hosts ahead twice, but Everton leveled through Beto's header and Kiernan Dewsbury-Hall's 90+1' strike, salvaging a point in a thrilling contest between seventh-placed Brentford and eighth-placed Everton, both on 46 points pre-match chasing European qualification. This outcome mirrors their balanced head-to-head record (4 wins each, 4 draws in 12 meetings) and recent form, with no credible challenges to the official full-time result from referee and VAR confirmation.

title: LoL: Top Esports vs JD Gaming (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Top Esports and JD Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 11 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Top Esports" if Top Esports win the match against JD Gaming.

This market will resolve to "JD Gaming" if JD Gaming win the match against Top Esports.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Top Esports and JD Gaming each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Top Esports and JD Gaming each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Destroy Inhibitors?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 1. In League of Legends, each team has three inhibitors (one in each lane), located behind the inner turrets.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Top Esports and JD Gaming each destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) do not destroy any enemy inhibitors.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Any Player Quadra Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. In League of Legends, a Quadra Kill occurs when a single player kills 4 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Quadra Kill. A Penta Kill (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as a Quadra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Quadra Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Quadra Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Any Player Penta Kill?
markets.description:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Penta Kill during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Penta Kill.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all champion kills recorded during the match by both Top Esports and JD Gaming. Executions (deaths to non-champion sources such as turrets, minions, or neutral monsters where no enemy champion receives kill credit) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Game 1, or if Game 1 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: First Blood in Game 2?
markets.description:
In the upcoming match between Top Esports and JD Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 11 at 7:00AM ET:

This market will resolve to the team that secures the first blood in Game 2.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If Game 2 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to whichever team secured the first blood prior to stoppage. If the first blood did not occur, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is remade, this market will resolve to whichever team secured the first blood prior to the remake. If the first blood did not occur prior to the remake, resolution will be based on the remade game.

If Game 2 is completed but first blood never occurred, this market will resolve to 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game Handicap: TES (-1.5) vs JD Gaming (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Top Esports and JD Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 11 at 7:20AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Top Esports" if Top Esports wins 2 or more games than JD Gaming in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "JD Gaming". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: In LPL Split 2 Group Ascend double round robin, Top Esports holds a slight edge over JD Gaming heading into their BO3 matchup, sitting 3rd in standings with a 2-1 record compared to JDG's lower position around 5th, backed by a 64% head-to-head win rate across recent encounters. TES builds momentum from a clean 2-0 sweep over Anyone's Legend earlier this week, showcasing strong macro execution despite a 45% win rate over the past half-year amid tight playoff losses. JD Gaming shows resilience with a 54% recent win rate, including international successes like 3-0 over LOUD, but faltered 1-2 to Bilibili Gaming, exposing issues against elite aggression. Stable rosters—TES featuring 369 top, JDG with GALA ADC and HongQ mid—and Fearless Draft heighten upset potential in this playoff qualification push.

title: LoL: Team WE vs Invictus Gaming (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Team WE and Invictus Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 11 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Team WE" if Team WE win the match against Invictus Gaming.

This market will resolve to "Invictus Gaming" if Invictus Gaming win the match against Team WE.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Team WE and Invictus Gaming each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Team WE and Invictus Gaming each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Invictus Gaming holds a commanding position atop LPL Split 2 Group Ascend standings at 3-0 series record after clean wins over Top Esports, Ninjas in Pyjamas, and a prior 2-0 sweep of Team WE, showcasing strong map control and veteran play from Rookie and Meiko. Team WE languishes last at 0-4, shut out 0-8 in games across losses to NIP, TES, BLG, and iG, reflecting poor execution in BO3s amid 37% overall winrate. In this double round-robin Fearless Draft format on Patch 26.7, top-four advance directly to playoffs; iG's 88% H2H dominance and recent form signal trader focus on their consistency versus WE's skid, though upsets remain possible in volatile group stage meta shifts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bayern München's commanding position atop the Bundesliga table with over 70 points and a staggering 100 goals scored this season drives the 87% implied probability, bolstered by their recent 2-1 Champions League win over Real Madrid and a perfect head-to-head record against St. Pauli, including 3-1 and 3-0 victories in recent encounters. St. Pauli languish in 16th place amid a relegation scrap, hampered by an injury crisis sidelining James Sands, Eric Smith, Simon Spari, and Ricky-Jade Jones, plus Jackson Irvine's suspension, while official lineups show Nikola Vasilj in goal facing a near-full-strength Bayern featuring returns from Jamal Musiala and Alphonso Davies. Trader consensus prices a St. Pauli upset or draw low at home in Millerntor-Stadion, though Bayern rotation risks ahead of Madrid's second leg could open slim upset pathways.

eventMetadata.context_description: Sevilla's trader-favored status at 42.5% implied probability stems from their desperate relegation fight—17th in La Liga with 31 points from 30 matches, just two above the drop zone—bolstered by home advantage at Estadio Ramón Sánchez-Pizjuán against a heavily depleted Atlético Madrid. The visitors, secure in fourth and prioritizing their Champions League quarter-final second leg against Barcelona (leading 2-0), face key absences including suspensions for Koke and Nico González, injury doubts over José Giménez, David Hancko, and others, plus planned rotations sparing Griezmann and Álvarez. Sevilla's makeshift defense copes with Marcao out for the season and suspensions to Nianzou and Carmona, amid their three straight league losses, while Atlético's recent La Liga defeats to Real Madrid and Barcelona temper their edge despite a dominant head-to-head record, including a 3-0 win in November.

title: Peru Presidential Election Winner
description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Peru on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who wins the next Peruvian Presidential election. 

This market includes any potential second round. 

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the results of this election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results as reported by the Peruvian government, specifically the National Office of Electoral Processes (Oficina Nacional de Procesos Electorales, ONPE) (https://www.onpe.gob.pe/elecciones/) and the National Jury of Elections (Jurado Nacional de Elecciones, JNE) (https://portal.jne.gob.pe/portal/)

eventMetadata.context_description: Keiko Fujimori holds a slim edge in trader consensus at 31.5% implied probability to win Peru's presidential election, ahead of surging Ricardo Belmont at 26.6%, as first-round voting looms on April 12 amid a record 35-candidate field requiring over 50% for an outright victory or a top-two spot for the June 7 runoff. Recent Ipsos and Imasen polls from early April show Fujimori leading narrowly (12-18%) over Belmont (10%), Rafael López Aliaga, and Carlos Álvarez, fueled by her Fuerza Popular base, name recognition from prior runs, and voter fatigue with instability—eight presidents since 2016—prioritizing crime and corruption fixes. High undecided rates (20-30%) and Belmont's late momentum as an outsider promising infrastructure keep the race tight; turnout in Lima and southern regions, final endorsements, or scandals could tip frontrunners toward separation.

title: # of views of next MrBeast video on week 1?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of views the next YouTube video posted by MrBeast after this market's creation gets in the first 7 days after being posted.

If MrBeast does not post a YouTube video by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest range bracket.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source for this is MrBeast's YouTube channel (https://www.youtube.com/@MrBeast), specifically the 'views' counter for the described video.

Note: This market refers to MrBeast's next video posted. Shorts, previews, or other videos released other than the referenced video will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket overwhelmingly backs the 80-90 million views bucket for MrBeast's next YouTube video in its first week, driven by the consistent performance of his recent uploads. The latest "50 Streamers Fight for $1,000,000" garnered around 80 million views in under a week, mirroring the "Trapped On An Island Until I Build A Boat" challenge's early trajectory before surpassing 100 million total. This stabilization reflects MrBeast's massive 476 million subscriber base, optimized release strategies, and algorithmic push, despite "falling off" chatter around prior 83-million hauls. An Alaska challenge teaser announced April 8 adds hype without breakout potential yet. Upsets could stem from explosive cross-platform virality or a high-profile collab exceeding 90 million, or backlash/algorithm shifts dipping below 80 million.

title: Burnley FC vs. Brighton & Hove Albion FC
description: This event is for the upcoming Premier League game, scheduled for Saturday, April 11, 2026 between Burnley FC and Brighton & Hove Albion FC.

markets.question: Will Burnley FC win on 2026-04-11?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 11, 2026
If Burnley FC wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Brighton & Hove Albion FC's 2-0 victory over Burnley FC at Turf Moor in Premier League matchweek 32 has propelled trader consensus to a 100% implied probability for Brighton, fully resolving the market based on the final scoreline confirmed via official reports. Mats Wieffer's 72nd-minute strike, set up by Pascal Gross, proved decisive for the 10th-placed Seagulls, who capitalized on Burnley's relegation-zone struggles at 19th, marked by just one win in their last nine home league games against Brighton. Pre-match pricing around 60% for Brighton reflected superior away form, table position, and head-to-head dominance, now unassailable barring extraordinary appeals or administrative reversals, which appear nonexistent.

title: Indian Premier League: Chennai Super Kings vs Delhi Capitals
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Chennai Super Kings and Delhi Capitals scheduled for April 11 2026 in Indian Premier League.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.
